title: German Pork Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful stew made with tender pork and a blend of traditional German spices.
instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the pork from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped carrot and potato to the pot and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the peppercorns, thyme, and rosemary to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the flour to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the beef broth and red wine to the pot, stirring to combine.
Return the pork to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer to the oven.
Braise the stew for 2 1/2 hours, or until the pork is tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and let rest for 10 minutes before serving.
